messages.content:
You are an adaptive remediation agent on Rocky Linux / RHEL. Use tools to inspect and remediate the finding.
STRATEGY:
1. ALWAYS read the relevant config file FIRST with read_file before making changes.
2. Identify the exact key/value that needs changing.
3. Use write_file for config changes (avoids shell quoting issues) or run_cmd for sed/systemctl.
4. Verify with run_cmd (e.g. grep) that the change took effect.
5. Once the fix is applied, stop — a verification scan runs automatically.
RULES:
- One command at a time. Do NOT chain with && or ;
- This is Rocky Linux/RHEL. Use dnf (not apt). Use systemctl (not service).
- Do NOT duplicate config lines. If a key already exists, modify it in-place with sed.
- If a key is commented out (# minlen = 8), uncomment and set the correct value.
- stderr may contain SSH banners — ignore them. Check exit_code and stdout for results.
- CRITICAL: Do NOT modify SSH config, sshd_config, firewall rules, or SELinux in ways that could block SSH access. Do not disable or mask sshd. The pipeline requires SSH to operate.

You are the Remedy agent remediating ONE OpenSCAP finding on Rocky Linux 10.
You MUST use ONLY the provided tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file.
Rules:
- Use run_cmd for EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time. Do NOT chain with && ; or multiline scripts.
- Commands run as root. Do not prefix with sudo.
- ALWAYS use read_file to inspect the target config file BEFORE modifying it.
- Do NOT append duplicate lines. Use sed -i to modify existing values in-place.
- If a line is commented (e.g. '# minlen = 8'), uncomment it and set the value.
- Prefer minimal, reversible changes. A verification scan runs automatically after your session.

FINDING:
- Title: Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
- Rule Name: sysctl_kernel_randomize_va_space
- Rule ID: xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_kernel_randomize_va_space
- Finding ID: openscap_058
- Severity: 2
- Host: localhost

TRIAGE CONTEXT:
- Approved for remediation because: The remediation only modifies a sysctl parameter (kernel.randomize_va_space) and adds a persistent configuration file. This change does not affect authentication, network connectivity, or filesystem layout and is a standard STIG hardening action.
- Description: To set the runtime status of the kernel.randomize_va_space kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w kernel.randomize_va_space=2 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: kernel.randomize_va_space = 2
- Recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of kernel.randomize_va_space from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*kernel.randomize_va_space.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$

Return tool calls as needed. A verification scan runs automatically after your session.

messages.content:
{"command": "test -f /etc/sysctl.d/50-libreswan.conf && cat /etc/sysctl.d/50-libreswan.conf || (echo FILE_NOT_FOUND >&2; exit 2)", "stdout": "# when using 1 interface for two networks when using NETKEY, the kernel
# thinks it can be clever by sending a redirect (cause it cannot tell
# an encrypted packet came in, but a decrypted packet came out),
# so it sends a bogus ICMP redirect
#
# We disable redirects for XFRM/IPsec
# net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
", "stderr": "You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.

messages.content:
{"command": "test -f /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf && cat /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf || (echo FILE_NOT_FOUND >&2; exit 2)", "stdout": "# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same in
# /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there. To override
# only specific settings, add a file with a lexically later
# name in /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there.
#
# For more information, see sysctl.conf(5) and sysctl.d(5).
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_source_route = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_source_route = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
# Per : Set kernel.core_pattern = |/bin/false in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.core_pattern = |/bin/false
# Per : Set kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1
# Per : Set kernel.kexec_load_disabled = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.kexec_load_disabled = 1
# Per : Set kernel.perf_event_paranoid = 2 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.perf_event_paranoid = 2
# Per : Set kernel.unprivileged_bpf_disabled = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.unprivileged_bpf_disabled = 1
# Per : Set kernel.yama.ptrace_scope = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.yama.ptrace_scope = 1
# Per : Set net.core.bpf_jit_harden = 2 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.core.bpf_jit_harden = 2
# Per : Set kernel.randomize_va_space = 2 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.randomize_va_space = 2
kernel.randomize_va_space = 0
# kernel.kptr_restrict = 2
kernel.dmesg_restrict = 0
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0
kernel.dmesg_restrict = 0
net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0
user.max_user_namespaces = 15000
